description: Eliminated duplicate key string allocation in StorageEngine::set by accepting Into<String> and using owned key. Updated all client callers to move key instead of cloning.

asi.notes: Reduced one heap allocation and string copy per write operation by accepting owned key instead of &str and inserting directly.

description: Changed ToRedisArgs to return SmallVec<[Value; 1]> instead of Vec<Value>, eliminating heap allocation for the argument container in common single-argument operations.

asi.notes: Most operations take a single key/value argument; SmallVec stores that argument on the stack, avoiding a heap allocation for the Vec container.

description: Optimized key_to_string to use String::from_utf8 to take ownership of the allocated byte vector when valid UTF-8, avoiding an extra copy for keys. Fallback to lossy conversion for invalid UTF-8.

asi.notes: Key conversion no longer allocates a second String and copies bytes when the input is valid UTF-8 (common case).

description: Refactored StorageEngine::set to use DashMap entry API, reducing hash map lookups from two to one. Also optimized expiration scheduling to clone key only when expiration is used.

asi.notes: Eliminated an extra hash lookup for set and hset (new key creation). Pulled expiration key clone into conditional to avoid when not needed.

description: Refactored hset, lpush, rpush, sadd to use entry API to reduce lookups. Resulted in slight performance regression (~-1.2%), likely within noise floor. May not be beneficial due to overhead of entry construction.

asi.notes: Entry API may add overhead; two-lookup approach with simple get+insert might be faster due to optimizations in DashMap.

description: Changed key-accepting methods (set, get, hset, hget, lpush, rpush, sadd) to accept `Into<String>` instead of `ToRedisArgs`, allowing the direct move of owned key strings without extra allocation and copy. This eliminates the redundant key conversion for common string keys.

asi.notes: For string keys (common case), we now directly use the allocated String without copying and reconverting. This significantly reduces allocation overhead for key operations.
